findings.title: Board-down e2e drives a stand-in voice flow, not the real REQ-5 spine
findings.description: AC16.2 requires running the full REQ-5 scenario with the board down. The e2e instead calls runBoardIndependentVoiceFlow(), a purpose-built helper in src/obs/board.ts that returns a fixed {ok,correlationId,stages}. The off-path RBG (requireBoard -> awaits board -> throws) is genuinely wired and fails red, and the real spine.e2e passes independently without importing the board, so the off-path guarantee holds in practice. But the 'matches the live run' assertion compares two hand-authored fixtures rather than an actual live run, so that specific claim is weakly proven. Consider driving the real spine flow with the board server down.

findings.title: OTLP trace/span IDs derived from a 32-bit FNV hash, not real OTel IDs
findings.description: fixedHex() folds correlationId/upid into a 32-bit FNV-1a value then pads/truncates to 32/16 hex chars, so trace/span IDs are deterministic but collision-prone and not genuine 128/64-bit OTel identifiers. Acceptable for the informational, non-blocking P-OTEL probe; would need real ID generation before relying on Langfuse trace correlation in production.
